comparison_to_v1: Noticeable improvement over v1 in prose coherence and paragraph-level structure. Paragraphs consistently open with topic sentences ('Our central finding is...', 'The results sharpen the understanding...', 'The parameter-level analysis provides a complementary lens...'). Transitions between Results subsections are explicit narrative bridges ('Before we could ask whether causal ECoG decoding is feasible...', 'Having established that...'). No visible \input{} seams - the voice is unified from Abstract through Conclusion. Discussion is 6 substantive paragraphs vs v1's shallower treatment, with proper framing (central finding, causal-non-causal interpretation, parameter-level interpretation, hemisphere implications, limitations, outlook). All key numbers (PCC 0.798/0.796/0.804, p-values 0.1022/0.2794/0.0007, N=48, n=16/32, hemisphere PCCs 0.790/0.781) match source exactly. Citations are well-integrated and contextually placed. Slight citation coverage could still improve (still ~20 refs vs denser literatures). Overall a solid bump over v1's 89.
brief_notes: Strong, unified manuscript with excellent narrative flow. All quantitative claims verify against extraction (spectrogram PCCs, Wilcoxon p-values, parameter-level PCCs, hemisphere subset sizes, 18 parameters, 50-word set, 400 trials, 125 Hz). Tables and figures are well-captioned with quantitative content. Minor: figures remain bar-chart/schematic style (not data-rich panels). Discussion is genuinely 6 paragraphs with clear rhetorical purpose each. The 'Having established that...' transition pattern keeps the Results narrative flowing. No seams between sections. Represents clear v2 uplift over v1 primarily in writing/clarity/presentation axes.
